events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 45,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Belt mistracking observed during active conveyor operation — root cause not yet isolated between off-center loading and idler skew",
    "Mistracking creates pinch-point and nip-point exposure at idlers, pulleys, and belt edges, violating OSHA 1910.212 machine guarding requirements",
    "Continued operation under mistracking can cause belt damage, structural damage to idler frames, and sudden belt failure creating projectile/entanglement hazards",
    "Root cause not determined — loading chute alignment and idler frame squareness both require verification before corrective action"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop the conveyor and lock out / tag out before any inspection or adjustment",
    "Inspect loading chute and transfer point alignment to determine if material is being loaded off-center",
    "Check idler frame squareness to conveyor centerline and verify idler roll alignment per CEMA installation tolerances",
    "Verify belt tension and pulley alignment as contributing factors to mistracking",
    "Correct root cause — adjust loading chute, re-align idlers, or re-square idler frames as determined by inspection",
    "Re-start conveyor and confirm belt tracks centered through full circuit before returning to production",
    "Ensure all nip points and pinch points remain guarded per OSHA 1910.212 during operation"
  ],
  "rationale": "Belt mistracking on an operating conveyor is an active safety hazard. The mistracking creates entanglement and pinch-point exposure at rotating components, which is a direct OSHA 1910.212 machine guarding concern. The root cause must be isolated — off-center loading requires chute adjustment while idler skew requires mechanical re-alignment — but either way the conveyor must be stopped and locked out immediately. The component is installed but defective in alignment/operation, and is non-compliant with guarding and safe-operation requirements while mistracking persists.",
  "references": ["CEMA", "OSHA 1910.212"]
}
